action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   12345678
  +--------+
 1|#......#|
 2|q...x...|
 3|..S.y.2.|
 4|#....p1.|
 5|...#....|
 6|##...#..|
 7|.#.#....|
 8|.A......|
  +--------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (3,3) at t=0, FACING N.
- You always have a facing direction (N, S, E or W). Each tick you make exactly one move:
  F    = step one cell forward, in the direction you are facing
  L    = turn 90 degrees left, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  R    = turn 90 degrees right, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  WAIT = do nothing for one tick
  There are no absolute moves: to go "north" you must first be facing N, then move F.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving F into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are (facing unchanged) and
     the tick is wasted. L, R and WAIT never bump.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. This applies even on
     ticks where you only turned or waited. Passing a guard mid-move is safe; only
     ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (3,3) facing N again, the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the
  cancelled moves' ticks are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (2,8): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (6,4); t mod 4=1: (7,4); t mod 4=2: (7,3); t mod 4=3: (7,4). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (1,2); t mod 8=1: (2,2); t mod 8=2: (2,1); t mod 8=3: (3,1); t mod 8=4: (3,2); t mod 8=5: (3,1); t mod 8=6: (2,1); t mod 8=7: (2,2). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (7,4) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (5,2) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (7,3) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (5,3) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 30 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 6 moves at a time; you have at most 5 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you are at
(2,2) facing N at t=0 and want to reach (3,2), which is to the east. Moving F now would
take you to (2,1) instead. The correct sequence is R (t=1, now facing E, still at
(2,2)), then F (t=2, now at (3,2)). If (3,2) were a gate that is closed at t=2, that F
would BUMP and you would have to WAIT for a tick when it is open.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AND the direction you
predict you will be facing AFTER your plan has executed. The referee records your
prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (3,3) facing N at t=0. My goal is to pick up Parcel 1 at (7,4) and Parcel 2 at (7,3), then deliver them to (5,2) and (5,3) respectively.
First, I need to navigate to the right side of the map where the parcels are.
Current position: (3,3). Target: (7,4).
Path planning:
1. Turn Right (R) to face East. (t=1)
2. Move Forward (F) to (3,4). (t=2)
3. Move Forward (F) to (3,5). (t=3)
4. Move Forward (F) to (3,6). (t=4)
5. Move Forward (F) to (3,7). (t=5)
6. Move Forward (F) to (3,8). (t=6)
Wait, let's re-evaluate the map layout.
Row 3: ..S.y.2. -> (3,3) is S. (5,3) is y (Parcel 2 delivery). (7,3) is 2 (Parcel 2 pickup).
Row 4: #....p1. -> (4,4) is wall? No, row 4 is `#....p1.`. Col 1 is #. Col 2-6 are dots. Col 7 is p (Parcel 1 pickup). Col 8 is 1 (Parcel 1 delivery? Wait, legend says 1=pickup, x=delivery. Map shows '1' at (8,4) and 'x' at (5,2)? Let's re-read carefully).
Legend: "1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell".
Map Row 4: `#....p1.` -> Col 1:#, 2:., 3:., 4:., 5:., 6:p, 7:1, 8:.
Wait, the map string is `#....p1.`.
Indices: 1:#, 2:., 3:., 4:., 5:., 6:p, 7:1, 8:.
So Parcel 1 Pickup is at (7,4). Parcel 1 Delivery is 'x'. Where is 'x'?
Map Row 2: `q...x...` -> Col 1:q, 2:., 3:., 4:., 5:x, 6:., 7:., 8:.
So Parcel 1 Delivery is at (5,2).
Map Row 3: `..S.y.2.` -> Col 1:., 2:., 3:S, 4:., 5:y, 6:., 7:2, 8:.
So Parcel 2 Pickup is at (7,3). Parcel 2 Delivery is 'y' at (5,3).

Okay, let's re-plan.
Start: (3,3) facing N.
Target: Get to (7,4) or (7,3).
Route:
t=0: (3,3) N
t=1: R -> (3,3) E
t=2: F -> (3,4) E
t=3: F -> (3,5) E
t=4: F -> (3,6) E
t=5: F -> (3,7) E
t=6: F -> (3,8) E
Now at (3,8). Need to go to (7,4) or (7,3).
Row 8: `.A......` -> (2,8) is Gate A.
Row 7: `.#.#....` -> Walls at (2,7), (4,7).
Row 6: `##...#..` -> Walls at (1,6), (2,6), (6,6).
Row 5: `...#....` -> Wall at (4,5).
Row 4: `#....p1.` -> Wall at (1,4).
Row 3: `..S.y.2.` -> Open.
Row 2: `q...x...` -> Open.
Row 1: `|#......#|` -> Walls at (1,1), (8,1).

Let's trace a path from (3,8) to (7,4).
From (3,8), I can go South?
(3,8) -> S -> (3,7).
(3,7) -> S -> (3,6).
(3,6) -> S -> (3,5).
(3,5) -> S -> (3,4).
(3,4) -> S -> (3,3). Back to start.
I need to go East.
From (3,8), go East to (4,8), (5,8), (6,8), (7,8), (8,8).
Then South?
(8,8) -> S -> (8,7) -> (8,6) -> (8,5) -> (8,4) -> (8,3) -> (8,2) -> (8,1).
At (8,4), I am at the same row as Parcel 1 pickup (7,4).
Let's check walls.
Row 8: `.A......` -> All open except (2,8) is Gate.
Row 7: `.#.#....` -> (2,7), (4,7) are walls. (8,7) is open.
Row 6: `##...#..` -> (6,6) is wall. (8,6) is open.
Row 5: `...#....` -> (4,5) is wall. (8,5) is open.
Row 4: `#....p1.` -> (1,4) is wall. (8,4) is open.
Row 3: `..S.y.2.` -> (8,3) is open.
Row 2: `q...x...` -> (8,2) is open.
Row 1: `|#......#|` -> (8,1) is wall.

So path:
Start (3,3) N.
1. R (face E)
2. F (3,4)
3. F (3,5)
4. F (3,6)
5. F (3,7)
6. F (3,8)
Now at (3,8) facing E.
7. F (4,8)
8. F (5,8)
9. F (6,8)
10. F (7,8)
11. F (8,8)
12. S (8,7)
13. S (8,6)
14. S (8,5)
15. S (8,4)
16. S (8,3)
17. S (8,2)
18. S (8,1) -> BUMP! (8,1) is wall.
Wait, I need to get to (7,4).
From (8,4), I can go West to (7,4).
So:
...
15. S (8,4)
16. W (face W)
17. F (7,4) -> Pickup Parcel 1.
18. F (6,4)
19. F (5,4)
20. F (4,4)
21. F (3,4)
22. F (2,4)
23. F (1,4) -> BUMP (Wall).
This path is getting long. Let's optimize.
I need to pick up P1 at (7,4) and P2 at (7,3).
Maybe go to (7,3) first?
From (3,8) -> ... -> (8,4) -> (8,3) -> (7,3).
Path:
(3,3) -> R, F, F, F, F, F (to 3,8). (6 moves)
(3,8) -> F, F, F, F, F (to 8,8). (5 moves)
(8,8) -> S, S, S, S, S (to 8,4). (5 moves)
(8,4) -> S (to 8,3). (1 move)
(8,3) -> W (face W), F (to 7,3). (2 moves)
Total moves so far: 6+5+5+1+2 = 19 moves.
At t=19, I am at (7,3) facing W. Pickup P2.
Then I need to go to (7,4) for P1.
(7,3) -> N (face N), F (to 7,4). (2 moves)
Total: 21 moves. Pickup P1.
Now I have both parcels.
Deliver P1 to (5,2).
Deliver P2 to (5,3).
Current pos: (7,4) facing N.
Need to go to (5,2) and (5,3).
Path from (7,4):
(7,4) -> W, F (6,4), F (5,4), F (4,4), F (3,4), F (2,4), F (1,4) [BUMP].
Wait, (1,4) is wall.
Let's look at the map again.
Row 4: `#....p1.` -> (1,4) is #.
Row 3: `..S.y.2.` -> (1,3) is ., (2,3) is ., (3,3) is S.
Row 2: `q...x...` -> (1,2) is q, (2,2) is ., (3,2) is ., (4,2) is ., (5,2) is x.
So to get to (5,2), I can go via Row 3 or Row 2.
From (7,4):
Option A: Go to (7,3) then up to (7,2) then left?
(7,4) -> S (7,3) [Pickup P2 if not done], N (7,4), W (6,4), W (5,4), W (4,4), W (3,4), W (2,4), W (1,4) [BUMP].
Option B: Go down to Row 3?
(7,4) -> S (7,3) -> S (7,2) -> S (7,1) [BUMP? Row 1 is `|#......#|`. (7,1) is .].
Row 1: 1:#, 2:., 3:., 4:., 5:., 6:., 7:., 8:#.
So (7,1) is open.
Path: (7,4) -> S (7,3) -> S (7,2) -> S (7,1).
Then W to (6,1), (5,1), (4,1), (3,1), (2,1), (1,1) [BUMP].
Then S to (1,2)? No, (1,2) is q.
Let's try to reach (5,2) and (5,3) from the right side.
From (7,4):
Go West along Row 4? (7,4)->(6,4)->(5,4)->(4,4)->(3,4)->(2,4)->(1,4) [BUMP].
Go South to Row 3? (7,4)->(7,3)->(7,2)->(7,1).
From (7,1) go West to (5,1)? (7,1)->(6,1)->(5,1).
From (5,1) go South to (5,2).
This seems viable.
Let's check guards.
Guard P: Cycle 4. t=0:(6,4), t=1:(7,4), t=2:(7,3), t=3:(7,4).
Guard Q: Cycle 8. t=0:(1,2), t=1:(2,2), t=2:(2,1), t=3:(3,1), t=4:(3,2), t=5:(3,1), t=6:(2,1), t=7:(2,2).

Let's refine the first plan to get both parcels.
Start (3,3) N.
Plan 1 (6 moves):
1. R (face E)
2. F (3,4)
3. F (3,5)
4. F (3,6)
5. F (3,7)
6. F (3,8)
Pos: (3,8) E.
Check guards at t=6.
P: 6 mod 4 = 2 -> (7,3).
Q: 6 mod 8 = 6 -> (2,1).
Safe.

Plan 2 (6 moves):
From (3,8) E.
1. F (4,8)
2. F (5,8)
3. F (6,8)
4. F (7,8)
5. F (8,8)
6. S (8,7)
Pos: (8,7) S.
Check guards at t=12.
P: 12 mod 4 = 0 -> (6,4).
Q: 12 mod 8 = 4 -> (3,2).
Safe.

Plan 3 (6 moves):
From (8,7) S.
1. S (8,6)
2. S (8,5)
3. S (8,4)
4. S (8,3)
5. S (8,2)
6. S (8,1) -> BUMP! (8,1) is wall.
Need to adjust.
From (8,7), go to (8,4) in 3 moves.
Then turn West?
Let's try:
1. S (8,6)
2. S (8,5)
3. S (8,4)
4. W (face W)
5. F (7,4) -> Pickup P1.
6. F (6,4)
Pos: (6,4) W.
Check guards at t=18.
P: 18 mod 4 = 2 -> (7,3).
Q: 18 mod 8 = 2 -> (2,1).
Safe.
I picked up P1 at (7,4).

Plan 4 (6 moves):
From (6,4) W.
1. F (5,4)
2. F (4,4)
3. F (3,4)
4. F (2,4)
5. F (1,4) -> BUMP.
Need to go down to Row 3 or 2.
From (6,4), go South to (6,3)?
Row 3: `..S.y.2.` -> (6,3) is .
Row 4: `#....p1.` -> (6,4) is .
Row 5: `...#....` -> (6,5) is .
Let's try to get to (7,3) for P2.
Current: (6,4) W.
1. R (face S)
2. F (6,3)
3. F (6,2)
4. F (6,1)
5. F (5,1)
6. F (4,1)
Pos: (4,1) S.
Check guards at t=24.
P: 24 mod 4 = 0 -> (6,4).
Q: 24 mod 8 = 0 -> (1,2).
Safe.
But I missed (7,3).
Let's backtrack.
From (6,4) W.
I need to get to (7,3).
(6,4) -> S (6,3) -> S (6,2) -> S (6,1) -> E (6,1) -> F (7,1) -> S (7,2) -> S (7,3). Too long.
Alternative: From (6,4) go East? No, I came from East.
From (6,4), go South to (6,3), then East to (7,3)?
(6,4) -> S (6,3) -> E (face E) -> F (7,3).
Moves:
1. S (6,3)
2. R (face E)
3. F (7,3) -> Pickup P2.
4. F (7,2)
5. F (7,1)
6. F (6,1)
Pos: (6,1) S.
Check guards at t=24.
P: 24 mod 4 = 0 -> (6,4).
Q: 24 mod 8 = 0 -> (1,2).
Safe.
I have P1 and P2.

Plan 5 (6 moves):
Deliver P1 to (5,2) and P2 to (5,3).
Current: (6,1) S.
Target: (5,2) and (5,3).
Path: (6,1) -> S (6,2) -> S (6,3) -> W (6,3) -> F (5,3) [Deliver P2] -> S (5,4)? No, (5,3) is y.
Wait, (5,3) is y. (5,2) is x.
From (6,1):
1. S (6,2)
2. S (6,3)
3. W (face W)
4. F (5,3) -> Deliver P2.
5. F (4,3)
6. F (3,3)
Pos: (3,3) W.
Check guards at t=30.
P: 30 mod 4 = 2 -> (7,3).
Q: 30 mod 8 = 6 -> (2,1).
Safe.
But I haven't delivered P1 to (5,2).
I need to deliver P1 (at 7,4) to (5,2).
And P2 (at 7,3) to (5,3).
In Plan 4, I picked up P2 at (7,3). I did not pick up P1 in Plan 4?
In Plan 3, I picked up P1 at (7,4).
So at end of Plan 3, I have P1.
In Plan 4, I picked up P2 at (7,3). So I have P1 and P2.
In Plan 5, I delivered P2 at (5,3).
Now I need to deliver P1 to (5,2).
Current pos: (3,3) W.
Need to go to (5,2).
(3,3) -> E (3,4) -> E (4,4) -> E (5,4) -> S (5,3) [Occupied by P2 delivery? No, P2 delivered. Is it blocked? "passable only while open" applies to gates. Parcels don't block usually unless specified. Assume passable.] -> S (5,2) [Deliver P1].
Path:
1. R (face E)
2. F (3,4)
3. F (4,4)
4. F (5,4)
5. S (5,3)
6. S (5,2) -> Deliver P1.
Total ticks: 6+6+6+6+6 = 30.
Perfect.

Let's double check the guard positions and collisions.
Plan 1 (t=0-5):
t=0: (3,3) N.
t=1: R. Pos (3,3) E. P(7,3), Q(2,1).
t=2: F (3,4). P(7,4), Q(2,2).

t=7: F (4,8). P(7,3), Q(2,2).
t=8: F (5,8). P(7,4), Q(2,1).

t=12: S (8,7). P(6,4), Q(3,2).
End Plan 2: (8,7) S. Safe.

t=12: (8,7) S.
t=13: S (8,6). P(7,3), Q(2,1).
t=14: S (8,5). P(7,4), Q(2
